title: Issues, Reviews, Pull Requests, and Team Workflow · Repository Trace: Workflow Policy
summary: Move a change from issue to review to integration with traceable decisions, bounded branch scope, and respectful collaboration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git workflow policy increment in a team contribution and review protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.
Respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Workflow Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Workflow Policy contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.

content.text: Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Workflow Policy condition decided your prediction.

content.value: gitrevisions Manual — Controls revision names, ancestry, range, exclusion, reflog, and object selection semantics.

steps.title: Bound Workflow Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Workflow Policy application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.

content.text: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy. A defensible solution applies Workflow Policy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a team contribution and review protocol.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 2
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: One branching model is best for every team and release cadence. This fails the Workflow Policy contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: gitrevisions Manual (current as of 2026-07) — Controls revision names, ancestry, range, exclusion, reflog, and object selection semantics.

steps.title: Sequence Workflow Policy evidence

steps.title: Build Workflow Policy evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-workflow-policy-zw2bol" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Workflow Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:

# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable cause]
# command: choose one bounded simulator observation
# observation: [replace with the result that changes the next decision]

steps.title: Repair the Workflow Policy defect
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

A teammate acts on this belief: “One branching model is best for every team and release cadence.” Which repair addresses the cause?

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Workflow Policy, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Workflow Policy model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Workflow Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Issue 2 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose trunk-based, feature-branch, release-branch, or fork collaboration from delivery, risk, ownership, and release constraints.
